title: RFDE: Resolution-Failure-Directed Extraction vs. CoT/RAG/LINC Baselines
layman_summary: A new AI reasoning method that fetches only the specific missing fact needed during logical inference, compared to three standard approaches on 26 question-answering tasks, achieving 100% accuracy at near-zero cost.
summary: Implemented and evaluated Resolution-Failure-Directed Extraction (RFDE), a neuro-symbolic pipeline where backward-chaining SLD resolution failures trigger on-demand LLM-based fact extraction from natural language documents. The pure-Python resolution engine (Term/Atom/Clause/unify/freshen) replaces SWI-Prolog for portability. On 26 tasks (8 synthetic, 8 CLUTRR-style family relations, 10 RuleTaker-style deductive), RFDE achieved 100% accuracy vs. CoT 96.2%, RAG+BM25 46.2%, LINC 84.6%. Hallucination rate: RFDE 0.0% vs. CoT 3.8%, RAG 53.8%, LINC 15.4% — 100% reduction vs. CoT baseline. Total LLM cost: $0.0025 (105 calls, well within $10 budget). Each task ran all 4 methods: (1) RFDE demand-driven grounding, (2) chain-of-thought LLM, (3) BM25 sentence retrieval + LLM, (4) LINC-style eager FOL translation. Confidence propagation via product rule along proof path. Proof traces captured as nested ProofNode objects and serialized to JSON. Method output file contains per-task predictions from all 4 methods plus an experiment summary dataset. Model used: meta-llama/llama-3.1-8b-instruct via OpenRouter. Python 3.12, uv venv, dependencies pinned in pyproject.toml.
